text:
Listen to another candidate doing the same task. As you listen, complete the script using the connecting words in the box. One of them can be used more than once.

and because but in spite of SO when who whose why

Well, the famous person I'm going to describe is David Beckham. He was a

explanation: یک کلمه کلیدی در موضوعات مربوط به ورزش و افراد مشهور در آیلتس.

text: 1[who] played for Manchester United, 2[and] he also played for England. I first saw him play for United 3[when] I was only seven, 4[so] that was

explanation: یک قید پرکاربرد برای بیان عدم قطعیت در اسپیکینگ آیلتس (Speculating).

text: in about 2002. I remember it as one of the most exciting days in my life! David Beckham became famous 5[because] he was a very

explanation: صفتی عالی برای توصیف ویژگی‌های مثبت افراد مشهور و موفق.
اشاره به نقطه قوت یا مهارت خاص یک فرد در انجام کاری.

text: was scoring amazing goals from free kicks. 6[But] he wasn't

explanation: مترادفی بسیار مناسب برای کلمه famous در آزمون آیلتس.

text: only for his football skills. He became even more famous when he married a pop star- Victoria, one of the Spice Girls. And another reason for his

explanation: اسم مشتق شده از صفت famous.

text: was his good looks and his style. I think almost every boy in the world wanted to have a David Beckham haircut! I know I did. I

explanation: نشان‌دهنده علاقه بسیار زیاد به کسی یا چیزی؛ قوی‌تر از like.

text: him as a boy 7[because] I was

explanation: یک اصطلاح غیررسمی (Informal) برای بیان علاقه شدید به یک سرگرمی یا موضوع.

text: football and he was my hero. The reason 8[why] I still like him is that 9[

explanation: یک حرف ربط سطح بالا (Connector) برای بیان تضاد؛ بعد از آن اسم یا عبارت اسمی می‌آید.
اشاره به نحوه برخورد و عمل کردن یک فرد.

text: like a normal guy. I heard a story from someone 10[whose] car was

explanation: یک فعل عبارتی (Phrasal Verb) به معنی از کار افتادن وسایل نقلیه یا ماشین‌آلات.

text: in the middle of the countryside. And then a car stopped and a man got out and asked him if he needed a push. It was David Beckham!\n\n
Use these words to make longer, connected answers for Speaking Parts 2 and 3.
